content.text:
<user_query>
You are QA lead for BIQc sprint execution. Build a practical test evidence matrix for technical and visual validation that can be executed fast. Return: (1) prioritized test matrix (persona x route x state), (2) pass/fail thresholds, (3) blocker definitions, (4) minimal artifact set for sign-off.
</user_query>
Exploring the codebase for routes, personas, and existing QA/sign-off conventions so we can produce a grounded test evidence matrix.

Here is a **practical BIQc sprint test evidence matrix** aligned with your repo routes (`App.js`), guards (`ProtectedRoute`, `LaunchRoute`), and the governance docs in `memory/FUNCTIONAL_SIGNOFF_GATES_2026.md` and `memory/SPRINT_EVIDENCE_PROTOCOL_2026.md`.

---

## 1) Prioritized test matrix (persona × route × state)

**Execution order:** run **Tier A** first (smoke); expand to **Tier B** for full sprint sign-off. Tag each run with `release_id`, environment, and build.

| Priority | Persona | Route(s) | States to capture (technical + visual) |
|----------|---------|----------|----------------------------------------|
| **A1** | Anonymous | `/`, `/login-supabase`, `/pricing` | success; hit protected URL → redirect to login (not loop) |
| **A2** | Free (authenticated) | `/soundboard` (post-login default per `PublicRoute`) | loading → success; nav shell renders |
| **A3** | Free | `/revenue` or `/reports` (any `LaunchRoute` paid) | **locked** → lands on `/biqc-foundation` (or `?feature=` as expected) |
| **A4** | Free | `/risk` or `/automations` (waitlist) | **locked** → `/more-features` (+ feature query if applicable) |
| **A5** | Paid (non-privileged) | same paid route as A3 | **success** (no paywall redirect) |
| **A6** | Paid | `/board-room` or `/marketing-intelligence` | success (validates another paid surface) |
| **A7** | Privileged | one waitlist route (e.g. `/war-room`) | **success** (bypass vs free user in A4) |
| **A8** | Any authenticated | `/onboarding` | success if incomplete; **exempt paths** still reachable from protocol (`/settings`, `/calibration`, etc.) |
| **A9** | Any | `/calibration` | success; **NEEDS_CALIBRATION** non-blocking per `ProtectedRoute` |
| **A10** | Admin | `/admin` | success after role check |
| **A11** | Non-admin | `/admin` | **access denied** (not crash; `AccessDenied` / `data-testid="access-denied-screen"`) |
| **B1** | Free + Paid | `/advisor`, `/market`, `/market/calibration`, `/alerts`, `/actions`, `/integrations`, `/settings` | loading → success; at least one **empty** or **degraded** where data APIs exist |
| **B2** | Paid | `/forensic-audit` or `/exposure-scan` | success |
| **B3** | Paid | `/email-inbox` or `/calendar` if sprint touched comms | success + error/degraded if integrations fail |
| **B4** | Admin | `/admin/prompt-lab`, `/support-admin`, `/observability` | success (admin-only parity) |
| **B5** | Public | `/trust/*`, `/biqc-legal`, `/biqc-foundation` (as legal/commercial) | success; copy matches availability (Commercial gate) |

**Fast “visual” subset:** For Tier A, capture **one viewport** per row (e.g. 1366×768) in **success** or **locked** state; add **mobile** (390×844) only for `/soundboard`, `/advisor`, `/login-supabase`.

**Technical checks per row:** HTTP/navigation outcome, console free of uncaught errors on happy path, API/edge failure → **degraded** not false success (maps to FM-01–FM-04 in `SPRINT_EVIDENCE_PROTOCOL_2026.md`).

---

## 2) Pass / fail thresholds

| Level | Pass | Conditional pass | Fail |
|-------|------|-------------------|------|
| **Sprint gate** (aligned with `FUNCTIONAL_SIGNOFF_GATES_2026.md`) | All **Tier A** rows pass; Tier B complete for **changed** scope; no open **P1** | Non-blocking **P2** with **named waiver + expiry + owner** (per gate policy) | Any **Tier A** failure; any **P1** open; entitlement drift (free sees paid; waitlist bypassed without privilege) |
| **Technical** | Expected navigation and API behavior; auth **no redirect loops** (20s grace in `ProtectedRoute`); admin routes match role | P2 issue with documented workaround | Crash, white screen, wrong tenant/data, broken paywall, security bypass |
| **Visual** | Critical chrome (nav, headings, CTA) within tolerance; no layout break on min viewports | Minor spacing/theme drift on non-critical surfaces | Illegible UI, broken nav, missing trust/error pattern on critical surfaces |

**Quantitative smoke (optional):** 100% of **Tier A** matrix rows pass; **0** P1; **≤ agreed** P2 with waivers.

---

## 3) Blocker definitions

Use the same severity language as `SPRINT_EVIDENCE_PROTOCOL_2026.md` / `FUNCTIONAL_SIGNOFF_GATES_2026.md`:

| Class | Definition |
|-------|------------|
| **Release blocker (P1)** | Core loop broken (auth → `/soundboard` → primary feature); wrong entitlements (free paid access, or paid blocked incorrectly); admin surface exposed to non-admin; redirect loop; data leak across users; calibration/onboarding hard-lock contrary to policy |
| **Gate blocker** | Missing mandatory evidence for a **must-pass** gate (technical E2E, visual pack, governance); unresolved **P1**; **Fail** gate state without approved process |
| **Non-blocker default** | **P3/P4** (can ship with tracked backlog); **P2** only with explicit waiver per gate rules |

**Sprint-specific blockers:** Any change to `LaunchRoute` / `tierResolver` / `routeAccessConfig` without updated **entitlement parity** row for all personas in scope.

---

## 4) Minimal artifact set for sign-off

Aligned with `SPRINT_EVIDENCE_PROTOCOL_2026.md` **§1.4, §2, §3** — minimal **pack**:

| Artifact | Contents |
|----------|----------|
| **1. Persona-route log** | Table: `release_id`, persona, route, expected vs actual URL, **pass/fail**, timestamp UTC, tester |
| **2. Flow matrix evidence** | FE-01–FE-08 (and FM-01–FM-05 where relevant) with **one line** result + link to log |
| **3. Visual pack** | Naming: `<release_id>_<persona>_<route_slug>_<viewport>_<state>.png`; cover **Core** routes from protocol + **paid** + **waitlist** + **admin** + **onboarding/calibration** |
| **4. Viewport proof** | At least: 1366×768, 1920×1080, 390×844 on **core** routes |
| **5. Defect list** | P1–P4, owner, **P1** = empty or all closed |
| **6. Governance snapshot** | Open risks (Critical/High), gate checklist (Technical / Visual / Governance), waivers with expiry |

**Smallest “fast sprint” cut:** Artifacts **1 + 3 (subset: Tier A routes only) + 5 + 6** — add **2** and full **4** when stakeholders require full protocol compliance.

---

**Reference paths:** `C:\Users\andre\.cursor\worktrees\tpn\fbn\frontend\src\App.js` (routes and `LaunchRoute`), `C:\Users\andre\.cursor\worktrees\tpn\fbn\frontend\src\components\ProtectedRoute.js` (auth/onboarding/calibration/admin behavior), `C:\Users\andre\.cursor\worktrees\tpn\fbn\memory\SPRINT_EVIDENCE_PROTOCOL_2026.md`, `C:\Users\andre\.cursor\worktrees\tpn\fbn\memory\FUNCTIONAL_SIGNOFF_GATES_2026.md`.
